scenario: Lattice Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS with 6800 claims per day. The operator entered the migration view from 120 active instances of claimsProcess version 3 and now needs to set the destination process and version before mapping any flow nodes from the source to the target on the cluster.
question: How does the operator pick the destination process and version in the migration view?

options.text: Enter the target process into the Target box and pick a version from the dropdown, defining where the selected source instances are migrated to in Operate
Drag the source process diagram tile onto the target diagram tile, which auto-selects the latest deployed version of the same BPMN file in the cluster setup
Pick the destination from the filters panel on the left, then click Bump Version to move the selection to the next published BPMN version on the cluster setup
Type the target version number into a dialog in the operations panel; the destination process id is then inferred from the source selection on the cluster setup

a.text: Correct. The migration guide says the operator enters a target process into the Target box and selects a version from the dropdown, which defines where all selected process instances are migrated to in Operate.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no documented drag-and-drop between the source and target diagrams; the destination is chosen via the Target box and version dropdown in the migration view, not by dragging tiles.

c.text: Incorrect. The filters panel chooses the source process and source version, not the destination; the destination is picked through the Target box and version dropdown in the migration view itself.

d.text: Incorrect. The destination process is explicit, not inferred, and is set through the Target box and version dropdown in the migration view, not through a dialog in the operations panel.

explanation: Per the migration guide, the operator types the target process into the Target box and chooses a version from the dropdown; this combination defines the process and version that the selected source instances are migrated to.
